title: cannot find module
author: LiamYaoLian

comments.author: skypesky
comments.body:
@LiamYaoLian 
hi, Now its highest version seems to be 4.3.0.
Please install with the latest version.
It may help you!
```shell
# modify version in package.json
"exceljs": "7.11.2" => "exceljs": "~4.3.0"
# install
npm install
# If this problem still occurs, please check if node_modules/ has an exceljs plugin
```

comments.author: LiamYaoLian
comments.body:
> @LiamYaoLian hi, Now its highest version seems to be 4.3.0. Please install with the latest version. It may help you!
> 
> ```shell
> # modify version in package.json
> "exceljs": "7.11.2" => "exceljs": "~4.3.0"
> # install
> npm install
> # If this problem still occurs, please check if node_modules/ has an exceljs plugin
> ```

Thanks very much! I tried to change it into "~4.3.0" and I checked node_modules as well. I got the following error when I ran the project. If I delete codes regarding importing exceljs, other codes work fine. 



> electron .

App threw an error during load
/Users/xxx-project/node_modules/readdir-glob/index.js:62
async function* exploreWalkAsync(dir, path, followSyslinks, useStat, shouldSkip, strict) {
              ^

SyntaxError: Unexpected token *
    at createScript (vm.js:74:10)
    at Object.runInThisContext (vm.js:116:10)
    at Module._compile (module.js:533:28)
    at Object.Module._extensions..js (module.js:580:10)
    at Module.load (module.js:503:32)
    at tryModuleLoad (module.js:466:12)
    at Function.Module._load (module.js:458:3)
    at Module.require (module.js:513:17)
    at require (internal/module.js:11:18)
    at Object.<anonymous> (/Users/xxx-project/node_modules/archiver/lib/core.js:9:12)

comments.author: skypesky
comments.body:
@LiamYaoLian 
```cmd
SyntaxError: Unexpected token * # Please check node.js version!One possibility is that your version is too old.

# If you need to solve your problem faster, I suggest you upload your project (of course, please remove your trade secrets) to github so that we can clone it for analysis
```
